Ad Text:
Company Description


Lendi Group is a market-leading digital platform business that is focused on revolutionising the entire property journey. It operates Lendi - Australia’s original and #1 digital mortgage brand - and the iconic Aussie franchise.

Powered by an advanced AI proprietary platform, a national network of 1,300 brokers, 230 retail stores, a team of experienced buyer’s agents, integrated property search and conveyancing, Aussie is supporting customers at every step of their property journey to find, buy and own with more confidence than ever before. Lendi Group’s loan book is more than $100b and it was named Australia’s Aggregator of the Year for 2024.

To learn more about life at Lendi Group check out our blog: https://www.lendi.com.au/inspire/category/life-at-lendi/

Innovation at Our Core – We challenge the status quo and push boundaries to create better solutions, with a goal of becoming AI-native by June 2026.
Work with the Best – Collaborate with some of the brightest minds in fintech, financial services, and strategy.
Make an Impact – Contribute to meaningful projects that shape our business and the future of property finance.
Grow & Evolve – Develop your skills and advance your career in a fast-moving, purpose-driven environment.

About the role:

The Platform Security Engineer is a valued member of our engineering team. We value evidence-backed opinions and offer the opportunity to play an integral part in shaping engineering rhythm, culture, and processes across planning, architecture and design, coding, deployment, and support.


In an ever-changing environment, Lendi Group is leading the charge in developing industry-first technology designed to offer Australian homeowners transparency, simplicity, and convenience in their home loan experience.
The Platform Security Engineer partners with engineering teams to evaluate planned changes, minimise security risk, and continuously improve our security posture. A key focus of the role is driving vulnerability and security findings down over time, improving security standards, and embedding secure-by-default practices across both traditional and AI-enabled systems.

Please note: this role will be a 12 Month Fixed Term Contract

Job Description


Working closely with the Cyber Security team to ensure security principles are consistently applied across the engineering environment.
Collaborating and coding with engineering teams to uplift secure development practices and reduce recurring security findings.
Designing, building, and maintaining secure cloud and microservice solutions.
Improving security posture through effective use of COTS products, platform controls, and network infrastructure.
Vulnerability and Threat Management — monitoring and assessing assets including cloud infrastructure, containers, packages, APIs, and requests, with a focus on remediation and measurable risk reduction.
Supporting the secure design and operation of AI-enabled systems, including LLM integrations, automation agents, and MCP-based architectures.
Implementing and reviewing AI security guardrails such as access controls, data protection, prompt-injection mitigation, and output validation.
Collaborating with cross-functional teams including engineering, operations, compliance, data, and AI platform teams.
Contributing as an individual contributor within a cross-functional DevOps / SecOps team.
Build or integrate services to improve security and compliance

Qualifications


The ideal candidate is passionate about promoting a security-first culture and has a strong, current understanding of security best practices, with particular focus on cloud security, secure design, incident handling, and operational resilience.
Experience securing microservices in cloud environments and working with configuration-as-code is important for this role. Experience securing AI-driven systems, including LLM-powered services and internal automation, is highly regarded.
Strong communication skills are essential. We value engineers who can clearly communicate risk, trade-offs, and remediation paths, and who approach collaboration with empathy for engineering challenges.

Key Skills

Strong understanding of networking, infrastructure, and applications from a DevOps perspective with a security focus.
Hands-on experience implementing and maintaining security controls across cloud environments (particularly AWS) and access management solutions (e.g. Auth0).
Experience with security logging, monitoring, and vulnerability assessment, with a demonstrated ability to drive findings down and improve baseline security standards.
Passion for secure coding practices and identifying common implementation and operational security risks.

Desirable Experience

AWS technologies such as CloudFormation, Terraform, IAM, WAF, Node.js and/or TypeScript.
Docker, containerised environments, serverless architectures, and Kubernetes.
Event-driven systems.
Securing AI platforms, including LLM integrations, MCP implementations, AI guardrails, and automation/build agents.

Additional Information


Lendi Group teams operate across Australia and the Philippines. Bringing together diverse expertise, innovative technology and a customer-first approach, our teams work seamlessly to simplify the property journey, helping Australians find, buy and own property with confidence.

We support our people in a variety of ways, but a few of the benefits that our people rave about include:

A vibrant, relaxed, yet professional culture.
Hybrid working arrangement designed to support work-life balance, while fostering meaningful connection and collaboration.
A holistic wellbeing programs offering 24/7 support, including medical, mental health, and financial wellbeing services to enable our workforce to thrive at home and work.
Generous paid Parental Leave: we celebrate our growing Lendi Group family with 18-26 weeks leave for primary carers and up to 4 weeks for secondary carers.
An additional week’s Loyalty Leave each year after reaching 3 years’ service.
Wellness initiatives with a strong focus on psychological safety.

We’re committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive community at Lendi Group. We believe that a team reflecting the world around us leads to greater innovation, stronger collaboration, and a more engaging workplace.

Our culture is guided by our 3 core values: We Are Stronger United; Act Like You Own It; and Keep Home Loans Human. Our values are part of our core DNA that helps Lendi Group to attract, engage and evolve the right talent and build best-in-class products.

This is an opportunity to shape the future of a fast-growing, purpose-driven company that’s transforming the homeownership journey.

Ready to contribute to Lendi Group’s next chapter? Apply now and be part of something big!

#lendigroup #LI-AMH1 #LI-hybrid

Job Title: Mid Level Software Engineer - AI Native Fintech
Ad Text:

Powered by an advanced AI proprietary platform, a national network of 1,300 brokers, 230 retail stores, a team of experienced buyer’s agents, integrated property search and conveyancing, Aussie is supporting customers at every step of their property journey to find, buy and own with more confidence than ever before. Lendi Group’s loan book is more than $100b and it was named Australia’s Aggregator of the Year for 2024.

To learn more about life at Lendi Group check out our blog: https://www.lendi.com.au/inspire/category/life-at-lendi/

Innovation at Our Core – We challenge the status quo and push boundaries to create better solutions, with a goal of becoming AI-native by June 2026.
Work with the Best – Collaborate with some of the brightest minds in fintech, financial services, and strategy.
Make an Impact – Contribute to meaningful projects that shape our business and the future of property finance.
Grow & Evolve – Develop your skills and advance your career in a fast-moving, purpose-driven environment.

About the role

Build the future of fintech — with AI at the core
Lendi Group is evolving into an AI-native fintech, embedding artificial intelligence into how we design, build, and operate our products. This isn’t about bolting on tools — it’s about empowering engineers to solve harder problems, move faster, and deliver smarter, more personalised experiences for millions of Australians.
We’re investing heavily in upskilling, experimentation, and modern engineering practices, giving our teams access to cutting-edge AI capabilities and the autonomy to shape how they’re used. If you’re curious, collaborative, and excited by what AI can unlock in real-world products — this is your moment.


We’re looking for a Mid Level Software Engineer to join our established team with high amount of BAU — You’ll work alongside passionate, experienced engineers tackling complex product and platform challenges, while helping define how AI is practically applied across our engineering lifecycle. You’ll have genuine influence — on architecture, engineering standards, delivery rhythm, and the evolution of an AI-enabled fintech platform — all in a team that values learning, ownership, and having fun while doing meaningful work.

Job Description


Own and deliver robust, scalable systems, influencing architecture and design decisions
Practise a true “you build it, you own it” mindset — from development to monitoring and support
Build clean, maintainable code with strong CI/CD practices and modern engineering standards

Work as a full-stack engineer, building backend services in Node.js (TypeScript) with React front-ends
Collaborate closely with Product Managers, Designers, BAs, and Engineers in cross-functional agile squads
Partner with Product and UX to shape intuitive customer journeys and interaction patterns
Drive team rhythm across planning, delivery, deployment, and continuous improvement
Mentor and uplift engineers across the team and broader engineering group
Apply AI tools thoughtfully across the SDLC to improve productivity, quality, and outcomes

Qualifications


Core experience
5+ years of backend development experience
Strong proficiency in Node.js (TypeScript) and modern backend ecosystems
Strong API design skills: REST, schema-first design, GraphQL, webhooks, and auth (Auth0, JWT)
Deep understanding of microservices architectures and messaging patterns (event-driven, pub/sub, request-response)

Hands-on experience with Kafka, Docker, and Kubernetes
Strong AWS experience (IAM, Lambda, DynamoDB, ECS, SSM)
Experience with DevOps and Infrastructure-as-Code approaches

AI & modern engineering (new and evolving)

Strong understanding of AI tools and their practical use across the SDLC
Experience or exposure to AI engineering, including:
Building Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines
Prompt engineering for effective LLM use cases
Working with LLM frameworks such as LangChain, Semantic Kernel, Agno, or similar
Exposure to agentic AI (AI agents, task orchestration, automation workflows)
Willingness to work across a variety of AI tools as the ecosystem evolves

Nice to have (but not essential)

Experience with Kotlin (used in parts of our Partner Product space)
Exposure to MongoDB, including schema design, indexing, and change streams
Experience streaming or integrating data into platforms like Databricks
Analytics and observability tools such as Amplitude and Datadog
Experience with Fastify (or similar Node frameworks) and Zod schema validation
Knowledge of Auth0 + OpenFGA and multi-client authentication flows
Understanding of security and compliance in an AI-enabled environment

Why Lendi Group?
Build real products used by millions, not experiments in isolation

Be part of a genuine AI-native transformation, not just “AI theatre”
Learn, experiment, and lead with modern AI and cloud technologies
Work with smart, supportive engineers who value ownership and craft
Shape the future of fintech while continuing to grow your own skills

If you’re excited by AI-enabled engineering, ownership at scale, and building meaningful fintech products — we’d love to hear from you.

Lendi Group teams operate across Australia and the Philippines. Bringing together diverse expertise, innovative technology and a customer-first approach, our teams work seamlessly to simplify the property journey, helping Australians find, buy and own property with confidence.

Generous paid Parental Leave: we celebrate our growing Lendi Group family with 18-26 weeks leave for primary carers and up to 4 weeks for secondary carers.
